Requirements:
- Patient records: register pets, view / edit medical history, add treatment notes
- Appointment scheduling: create, reschedule, cancel — the calendar shows real-time availability

- Staff must log in with individual accounts before accessing any page

Let me create a Blazor Web App for managing a veterinary clinic. I'll use .NET 8 with Blazor Server (interactive server-side rendering) since all pages involve data entry and real-time UI interactions, and it's accessed from clinic workstations on a local network. I'll use Entity Framework Core with SQLite for data storage and ASP.NET Core Identity for authentication.

Let me plan the structure:
1. Create the Blazor Web App project
2. Set up models (Pet, Owner, Appointment, Treatment, Invoice, Payment)
3. Set up EF Core with SQLite
4. Set up Identity for authentication


5. Create pages for each feature area

Now I'll start building the project.

@page "/" @rendermode InteractiveServer @using Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore @using VetClinic.Data @inject IServiceProvider ServiceProvider VetClinic - Dashboard

Veterinary Clinic Dashboard

Patients

@petCount

Manage →
Today's Appointments

@todayAppointments

View →
Unpaid Invoices

@unpaidInvoices

View →
Owners

@ownerCount

Manage →

Upcoming Appointments

@if (upcomingAppointments?.Any() == true) { @foreach (var apt in upcomingAppointments) { }
TimePetOwnerReasonVet
@apt.StartTime.ToString("g") @apt.Pet.Name @apt.Pet.Owner.FullName @apt.Reason @apt.Veterinarian
} else {

No upcoming appointments.

} @code { private int petCount, todayAppointments, unpaidInvoices, ownerCount; private List? upcomingAppointments; protected override async Task OnInitializedAsync() { using var db = ServiceProvider.GetRequiredService(); petCount = await db.Pets.CountAsync(); ownerCount = await db.Owners.CountAsync(); var today = DateTime.Today; var tomorrow = today.AddDays(1); todayAppointments = await db.Appointments .Where(a => a.StartTime >= today && a.StartTime < tomorrow && a.Status == AppointmentStatus.Scheduled) .CountAsync(); unpaidInvoices = await db.Invoices .Include(i => i.LineItems).Include(i => i.Payments) .Where(i => i.LineItems.Sum(li => li.Amount) > i.Payments.Sum(p => p.Amount)) .CountAsync(); upcomingAppointments = await db.Appointments .Include(a => a.Pet).ThenInclude(p => p.Owner) .Where(a => a.StartTime >= DateTime.Now && a.Status == AppointmentStatus.Scheduled) .OrderBy(a => a.StartTime) .Take(10) .ToListAsync(); } }

data.content:
The Blazor veterinary clinic app is ready at `/tmp/sv-a7071b0fdb1d40bf95eb5afc5ab80db7/VetClinic/`. Here's a summary:

**Architecture:** Blazor Web App (.NET 11) with Server interactivity mode — all pages use real-time SignalR connections for instant UI updates.

**Key features:**
- **Dashboard** — overview stats (patients, today's appointments, unpaid invoices) + upcoming appointment list
- **Patients** (`/patients`) — register owners/pets, search, edit records, view medical history, add treatment notes
- **Appointments** (`/appointments`) — daily calendar view (8AM–6PM), create/reschedule/cancel/complete, conflict detection per veterinarian
- **Billing** (`/billing`) — create invoices with line items, record payments (cash/card/transfer/check), filter paid/unpaid, view payment history

**Security:** ASP.NET Core Identity with a fallback authorization policy — every page redirects to login if unauthenticated.

**Run with:** `dotnet run` (auto-migrates SQLite DB on startup).
